name: Chocolate Almond Flourless Torte
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces good imported bittersweet chocolate, broken into pieces
1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter or margarine, at room temperature
1/2 cup granulated sugar plus more for sprinkling
1/3 cup finely ground almonds (done in a food processor)
2 tablespoons kosher for passover cognac or dark rum whipped cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300 degrees and grease well a 9-inch springform pan. Line the bottom with parchment paper.
2. Melt the chocolate in a double boiler over hot, not boiling water. When melted, turn off the heat and let cool slowly.
3. In a large bowl, beat the butter with 1/4 cup of sugar until fluffy and almost white. Add egg yolks and beat for 1 minute. Add almonds and cognac; beat for another 2 minutes.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until light and foamy. Gradually add remaining 1/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff and shiny.
5. Add melted chocolate to the yolk mixture and mix with a spatula until well combined. Fold in 1/4 of this chocolate mixture into egg whites; then gradually fold back into the rest of the chocolate mixture, taking care not to deflate it.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until a tester comes out with a thick, moist coating that is not wet or crumbly. Allow to cool for 30 minutes in the pan.
7. Loosen edges with a knife, carefully turn cake onto plate, remove parchment paper, sprinkle with sugar, and serve at room temperature or chilled with whipped cream and raspberries on side.
Yield: 10-12 servings
